tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with low urine specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal). These findings suggest significant renal impairment, possibly with tubular dysfunction, and possible underlying metabolic disturbance. HbA1c is low, arguing against chronic hyperglycemia. Further nephrological and metabolic evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
